item.title: Re: A few subtle details
item.content:encoded:
"As I understand it, inertial mass and gravitational mass have been found to be exactly equal in all cases, which very strongly implies that they're the same thing, but we don't 100% understand why they're the same thing."

Yes. Which is why you wouldn't be qualified to work with this thing. It breaks the laws of physics, but if you know why it does, it's gonna think you're awfully delicious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, I really do. Not only does the writer know and use quantum physics, they use insect behavior too.

I don't know why it doesn't have more upvotes; the idea of a bug with a very simple mind that changes based on how it's observed is pretty neat. And the idea of quantum effects taking place on a macroscopic scale and NOT being averaged out the way they usually are is pretty freaky. I'd love it if somebody wrote an experiment log—maybe that document that's referred to about the bug's abilities.

item.title: A few subtle details
item.content:encoded:
Its inertial mass is approximately 1700 kilograms, but its gravitational mass is approximately 375 grams

As I understand it, inertial mass and gravitational mass have been found to be exactly equal in all cases, which very strongly implies that they're the same thing, but we don't 100% understand why they're the same thing.

spraying its front pair of antenna with a 0.5% solution of methanoic acid.

"Methanoic acid", also known as "formic acid". The smell of ants. If you're an isopod, you… well, you don't hate the smell of ants, it doesn't terrify you, because you're not capable of that level of emotion. But it certainly triggers your 'move in the opposite direction, quick' response, especially if it's being sprayed right on your antennae.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a very bad article to read at 12:53 AM. Tentative downvote for being unengaging and technobabble filled, will revise when I wake up.

E: Reasonably well rested, article doesn't seem any better. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
such that research to determine whether SCP-074 has a given property or capability, results in SCP-074 developing or manifesting that property or capability.

I'm in love.

item.title: Staff Post - Closed
item.content:encoded:
Backing up sirpudding. This is not a RP wiki, and roleplaying is not allowed in the forums.

This is a closed staff post. Do not reply unless you are staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No roleplaying in the forums, please.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, the reality where i come from has these as pets, and we often ride them around like how you humans ride horses. Although this one is fairly similar, it has a few abilities unseen in our reality, mainly the parasitic form of reproduction and the quantum portal effect that it has. If you really want to keep it happy then give it a light spherical object, like a beach ball. They love to play with those.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Welp, I couldn't work with this thing. Love the use of "wavepacket" - great acknowledgement that light is damned funny in how it behaves.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find it somewhat difficult to believe that personnel with little knowledge regarding physics could be found and implemented when considering what the personnel are expected to do upon containment breach.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Foundation accepts that by having reasonably intelligent and educated personnel in proximity to 074, it will continue to manifest a certain non-zero amount of superpowers. However, the additional difficulty posed by these superpowers — given that 075 is wholly nonsapient — is, overall, less than the additional difficulty that would be posed by having all the containment personnel be totally uneducated idiots.

That said, anyone who's ever taken college-level physics probably could make this thing a lot more dangerous. And the skin sheets are cultured from samples taken from totally uneducated idiots.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it can only be kept by totally uneducated idiots?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Has anyone seen SCP-009-J?
Cause that page posts what is (according to the comments section) a picture of this thing.
Well done, though. I applaud the Quantum Woodlouse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I need to stay WAAAY far away from this thing. Which is sad, because i love it. Very well done! The physics is great, and fantastically entertaining.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The purpose of the skin is to intercept all the egg wavepackets.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love this.

I vaguely remember the old one; vastly improved. I didn't quite catch why skin is part of the containment, but hey… must not be cleared for this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's "ovipositor" in English: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ovipositor

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: no it's not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I haven't decided on a vote yet, but I want to point out a minor misspelling, "ovopositor" is spelled "ovipositor"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a rewrite of the old 074, which was also a Quantum Woodlouse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just curiousity, what was 74 before this? I'm also suprised when a new scip comes up in the main series 1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a giant bug that can teleport and reproduce through radiation.
Honestly, I wanted to complain about it but now that I say that, it seems pretty badass.
Take my +1 or whatever.

item.title: Re: PROVENANCE
item.content:encoded: It's either the sugary version of SCP Light or the thicker version of SCP Ultra-thin.

item.title: Re: PROVENANCE
item.content:encoded: …Dare I be noob enough to ask what or where SCP Classic is?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Aren't you supposed to be a snake?"
"Well, uh, it's a little complicated."

Regardless, the above explanation is utterly awesome, just pity that the article doesn't support it. That said, if you went and wrote it into a tale somehow, I'd upvote the hell out of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So it's a woodlouse, the size of a small car, whos anomalous properties consist solely of, somehow, using an outside sources' knowledge of physics. I presume only for the purposes of being a car-sized woodlouse(as in, it just wants to grow, make children, rinse and repeat until dead). That…that honestly sounds like something tailor made. You can only keep it where it is with, erm, ignorance. Of physics. Which also means, until whatever poor bastards figure out that their own knowledge of their profession is making it -better- at what it does, is in a hopeless struggle. The more it does something it should not be able to do, the more you will research to try and find out how in the Nine Worlds it-

…

Wait. Wait.

Woodlouse. Wood. Tree. Nine Worlds. World Tree. Yggdrasil.

Woodlice are found in damp places, because, well, they're crustaceans, and lose water quickly. What's normally the wettest place of a tree? The roots.

…is this Niddhogg? Or is my obsession with Old Norse making me see things that aren't there?

Regardless, upvoted. I liked it -before- I made this assumption, and I no longer care about the "tailor made" angle. Now I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "I don't know you. I don't know anything. You know physics so I encyst on you."

item.title: PROVENANCE
item.content:encoded: This was inspired by my own previous rewrite of a significantly-different-but-still-recognizable version (available on SCP Classic). That version was written by an unknown author on EditThis, and ported over by far2 during the migration.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure what I think of it because the style is just not working for me, at least at the moment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay…so how big is this thing? You say 1.7 cubic meters, and then say the size of a compact car. But 1.7 cubic meters would be a little more than 3x3x5 feet. Which would be a -really- compact car.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I can appreciate the cleanup of this article, I have downvoted it for reasons I explained while talking to you on IRC; I understand the concepts you're conveying but I heavily disagree with your terminology and think you can do better, especially considering your insistence that this is your field of expertise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Somewhere out there in the depths of 4chan, someone will actually draw that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, this is really nifty. It's like someone made this specifically to fuck with physicists.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Bad bug! BAD BUG! Get back into your cell now!" *pushing it like a stubborn cow*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Rather meh on the way this one is done, sorry - doesn't quite do enough with the bits I thought were actually neat.

That said, I do utterly love this sentence

In the event of a spontaneous containment breach, personnel can coerce SCP-074 into returning to its containment by first occupying each of its four sets of jaws with an entire raw apple, then physically pushing SCP-074 in the desired direction, gently tapping its compound eyes with an open palm, or spraying its front pair of antenna with a 0.5% solution of methanoic acid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Beautiful tone, exquisite idea. Also, replete with footnote which I love as it adds to verisimilitude.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really neat.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like what you've done with this, Voct.
+1.
